zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Grey於1201年12月1日出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾可德諾時，他的父親Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor25歲，母親Isolda Bardolf19歲。他和Lucia de Humet至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1271年9月8日在英國德比夏爾可德諾享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭德比夏爾可德諾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Grey于1201年12月1日出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾可德諾时，他的父亲Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor25岁，母亲Isolda Bardolf19岁。他和Lucia de Humet至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1271年9月8日在英国德比夏爾可德諾享年69岁，埋葬在英国英格兰德比夏爾可德諾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Grey was born on 1 December 1201, in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor, was 25 and his mother, Isolda Bardolf, was 19. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Lucia de Humet. He died on 8 September 1271, in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 69, and was buried in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Grey est né le 1 décembre 1201, à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Isolda Bardolf, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Lucia de Humet. Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1271, à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Codnor, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Grey am 1. Dezember 1201 in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor 25 und seine Mutter Isolda Bardolf 19. Er hatte mit Lucia de Humet mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 8. September 1271 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Codnor, Derbyshire, England und wurde in Codnor, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Grey è nato il il 1 dicembre 1201 nel Codnor, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Isolda Bardolf, aveva 19 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Lucia de Humet. Morì il 8 settembre 1271, nel Codnor, Derbyshire, England, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Codnor, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Greyが1201年12月1日にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーで生まれた時，父Henry de Grey Lord of Codnorは25歳，母Isolda Bardolfは19歳であった。彼はLucia de Humetとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1271年9月8日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーで69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアコドノーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Grey(이)가 1201년 12월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 코드너에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Isolda Bardolf(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Lucia de Humet(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1271년 9월 8일 영국 더비실르 코드너에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 코드너에 묻혔다.

pt.1 December 1201–8 September 1271: 1 de dezembro de 1201–8 de setembro de 1271
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Grey nasceu em 1 de dezembro de 1201, em Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Isolda Bardolf, tinha 19 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Lucia de Humet. Ele faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1271, em Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Grey родился 1 декабря 1201 г. в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry de Grey Lord of Codnor было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Isolda Bardolf, 19. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Lucia de Humet. Он умер 8 сентября 1271 г. в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности коднор, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Lucia de Humet. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1271, en Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Codnor, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
